cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: AI adoption hits 90% in CX but organizations split on architecture and governance
stories.plain: A new report finds nine in ten CX teams now use AI, but they are taking sharply different paths on how to build it, govern it, and prove it earns its keep. The divergence matters because early architectural choices lock in who controls decisions, how explainable they are, and how much customers trust the brand.

stories.head: Omneky launches public API and MCP server so AI agents can generate ad creative autonomously
stories.plain: Omneky has opened its ad-creative engine to external developers and AI agents via a public API and Model Context Protocol server. Any platform can now feed brand data in and receive ready-to-run creative out, making fully autonomous ad production a plug-in capability rather than a locked product. This reshapes how creative is bought and built.

stories.head: ZoomInfo releases open-source GTM.AI CLI giving AI agents direct access to verified B2B data
stories.plain: ZoomInfo's new command-line client lets developers and AI agents query verified go-to-market data directly from any terminal, no GUI required. This lowers the barrier for embedding accurate prospect and company data into agentic sales and marketing workflows, changing how GTM data is accessed and priced.

stories.head: Crunchbase CRO: siloed proprietary data — not AI model quality — is the real ROI blocker
stories.plain: Crunchbase's chief revenue officer argues that chasing a better AI model is a distraction — the actual barrier to returns is fragmented, proprietary data locked in silos. The interview offers a grounded view of why data strategy, not model selection, should come first when buying or building marketing AI.

stories.head: Kyles.tools launches free tracker showing how ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity answer questions about any brand
stories.plain: A new free tool lets small businesses monitor how AI search engines describe and recommend their brand across ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity, with built-in guidance rather than raw dashboards. As AI-generated answers replace traditional search results, knowing your brand's presence inside those answers is becoming a basic marketing need.

stories.head: AI adoption hits 90% as CX deployment paths diverge
stories.plain: A new report finds nearly every CX organization has adopted AI, but they are splitting sharply on architecture choices, governance frameworks, and how to build customer trust while proving ROI. This matters because the gap between adoption and reliable performance is now the central buying and building challenge for marketing AI leaders.

stories.head: Omneky Launches Public API and MCP Server for Autonomous Ad Creative Generation
stories.plain: Omneky opened its autonomous creative engine to external developers and AI agents via a public API and Model Context Protocol server. Any platform can now feed brand inputs and get back ad-ready creative. This shifts ad production from a human workflow to a composable, agent-callable service — a structural change in how creative is built and bought.

stories.head: Pega's Don Schuerman on the uncomfortable truth between AI ambition and AI reality
stories.plain: Pega's chief technology officer argues that most AI pilots fail not because models are weak but because underlying processes were designed for a pre-AI era. He outlines what agentic decisioning actually requires to produce CFO-pleasing results. Practical framing for anyone trying to move from AI experiments to production-grade marketing systems.

stories.head: ZoomInfo Launches Open-Source GTM.AI CLI for Verified Go-to-Market Data
stories.plain: ZoomInfo released an open-source command-line client that lets developers and AI agents query verified GTM data directly from any terminal. This makes clean, structured company and contact data a programmable input for agentic pipelines — closing a key gap between data quality and autonomous outreach systems.

stories.head: Multiply Launches '10 Minute ABM' to Deploy Hundreds of Personalized Ads for Top Accounts
stories.plain: Multiply's new product compresses what used to be 100 hours of account-based creative work into ten minutes by generating hundreds of 1:1 personalized ads per account with self-learning optimization. It signals how generative AI and bandit-style learning are collapsing the economics of ABM personalization at scale.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Agents Are Eating the Stack
body: Autonomous agents are no longer demos — they are shipping as production infrastructure. Omneky's MCP server lets any AI agent call ad-creative generation as a service, ZoomInfo's CLI pipes verified GTM data straight into command-line workflows, and Globant's AI Pods move enterprises from agentic prototypes to full deployment. The pattern is the same everywhere: capability that once required a human operator is being wrapped as an API and handed to another machine.

This collapses the traditional martech stack from a collection of dashboards into a set of callable services. Decisions about what to buy, build, or integrate now hinge less on UI quality and more on whether a tool exposes a reliable, agent-friendly interface. Vendors without an API-first or MCP-compatible surface risk being bypassed entirely as orchestration layers multiply.
ev: Omneky · autonomous ad creative via MCP
ZoomInfo · GTM data at the command line
Globant & Vercel · agentic AI to production

themes.head: AI Search Rewrites Visibility Rules
body: When AI answers replace ten blue links, the metrics marketers have relied on for two decades — click-through rate, keyword rank, organic sessions — begin to lose their meaning. The month's coverage makes explicit that trust signals, cited sources, and brand authority are now the primary levers for whether an AI system surfaces your content at all. Constructor's top Gartner rankings in AI-driven product discovery reinforce that the same shift is happening inside e-commerce search.

This is a strategic inflection, not a channel update. Budgets and measurement frameworks built around traditional SEO must be rethought from the ground up. Marketers who wait for standard analytics to show the decay will already be invisible to a growing share of AI-mediated buyer journeys.
ev: MarTech · trust as visibility signal in AI search
MarTech · AI discovery changes everything measured
Constructor · Gartner #1 in AI product discovery

themes.head: The ROI Reckoning Has Arrived
body: Ninety percent AI adoption in CX is the headline, but the real story is the divergence in how teams are deploying it and whether they can prove it pays. Crunchbase's CRO frames the pressure bluntly: boards that funded AI experimentation are now demanding numbers, and most marketing orgs cannot produce them. Rising customer-acquisition costs alongside stagnant deal quality suggest that AI is being applied to volume and speed — the easy parts — rather than the strategic judgment calls that actually move revenue.

The Pega conversation about the gap between AI ambition and AI reality lands in the same place: activity metrics (prompts run, content pieces generated) are not business metrics. The teams winning the ROI argument have instrumented outcomes, not outputs, and they have been ruthless about picking the two or three workflows where AI changes a decision, not just accelerates a task.
ev: Agile Brand · Crunchbase CRO on AI ROI pressure
MarTech · AI adoption at 90%, CX paths diverge
Agile Brand · Pega's Don Schuerman on AI ambition vs reality

themes.head: CMS & Content Infrastructure Become the OS
body: The content management system is quietly being repositioned as the orchestration layer for AI-generated brand output. When AI can produce video, copy, and personalized ads at scale, the system that governs what gets published, to whom, and with what guardrails becomes the most consequential piece of infrastructure a brand owns. PixPix's AI video editing for e-commerce and Beamr's ML-safe video data are both symptoms of this: content production is commoditizing, but content governance is not.

This reframes the CMS purchase decision entirely. Buyers should now evaluate a CMS the way they evaluate a data platform — on its ability to enforce brand standards, feed structured content to AI agents, and maintain an auditable record of what was generated and why. Vendors still selling on editorial UX alone are pitching the wrong value proposition.
ev: MarTech · CMS as AI operating system
PixPix · AI video editing for e-commerce
Beamr · ML-safe video data on RTMaps

themes.head: The Talent Pipeline Is Breaking
body: If AI handles the executional work that used to train junior marketers — writing briefs, pulling reports, testing copy — the apprenticeship model that has built marketing talent for decades quietly disappears. The question is not whether AI takes jobs; it is whether the learning loops that develop judgment are being automated away before organizations notice. The vibe-coding piece makes the same structural argument for technical marketers: speed without scaffolding produces fluency without understanding.

This is a slow-moving risk that feels invisible until it is acute. Leaders who rely on AI to accelerate output without redesigning how junior staff build skills will face a competency cliff in two to three years. The organizations getting ahead of it are deliberately creating AI-assisted but human-reviewed workflows, preserving the feedback cycles that turn task completion into professional judgment.
ev: MarTech · scaling vibe coding beyond better prompts
MarTech · scaling the wrong part of GTM with AI

month.takeaway: Across every category this month, the defining pressure is the same: AI is moving from accelerating existing workflows to restructuring the infrastructure, measurement, and talent systems underneath them — and most organizations are still optimizing the surface while the foundation shifts.
